summary: A symmetrical ceremonial or decorative structure featuring a central stepped pyramid roof flanked by four corner towers. The building uses brick walls with white concrete/quartz accents at the base and horizontal stripe detailing. The design emphasizes vertical elements with prominent corner pillars and a tiered roof system.

shape.overall_form: Square footprint with central pyramid and four corner towers

rebuild_hints: Construct four corner towers at each corner, extending upward 8-10 blocks
Create central pyramid roof with multiple stepped layers descending outward
Use brick as primary wall material with horizontal stripe pattern using lighter material
Add concrete or quartz blocks as base trim running around perimeter
Top each corner tower with overhanging dark wood slabs forming a cap
Use dark wood slabs for all roof layers in stepped pyramid pattern
Maintain strict four-way symmetry throughout construction
Add small gold block accents on tower caps
uncertainties: Exact interior layout not visible from exterior views
Precise block count for roof layers difficult to determine from angles shown
Whether structure has interior rooms or is primarily decorative
Exact material of horizontal stripes - could be stonebrick, concrete, or quartz
Height of corner towers relative to central structure may vary by 1-2 blocks
